title: The assessment tasks provided below uses a simulated business website named Career Training. To access the website, you …

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
The assessment tasks provided below uses a simulated business website named Career Training. To access the website, you need to log in by using the below-provided link:

http://careertraining.vetadvisorygroup.com/Links to an external site.

Username: CareertrainingRTO

Password: Career123

Career Training is acknowledged as an industry training leader delivering nationally recognised and specialised courses. Career Training is a private Registered Training Organisation (RTO) based in Geraldton, Western Australia, with satellite offices in Broome, Derby and Perth. 

Registered Training Organisations (RTOs) are those training providers registered by ASQA (or, in some cases, a state regulator) to deliver vocational education and training (VET) services.

RTOs are recognised as providers of quality-assured and nationally recognised training and qualifications.

Only RTOs can:

Deliver nationally recognised courses and accredited Australian Qualifications Framework (AQF) VET qualifications,
Apply for Australian, state and territory funding to deliver vocational education and training.
RTOs can offer qualifications at the following levels:

Certificates I, II, III and IV
Diploma
Advanced Diploma
Vocational Graduate Certificate
Vocational Graduate Diploma.
YOUR TASK:

Career training is aware that Covid-19 impacts all businesses in every industry. 

The organisation has planned to perform a risk management process to identify the risks associated with their organisation and treat those risks, accordingly.

The different risks the organisation will associate with the Covid 19 Risk Assessment is are highlighted in the following areas:

Staff shortage
Financial loss
Discrimination
Communication
You need to refer to the policies and procedures available on the Career Training website to complete the assessments as listed below:

Communication policies
Privacy and confidentiality policy and procedure
Harassment, grievance and complaints handling

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Risk Management Task:

1. Using the Career Training website, identify the different communication policies and procedures in place to manage the risks associated with Covid-19.

2. Evaluate the current privacy and confidentiality policy and procedure in place at Career Training to determine if it adequately addresses the risks associated with Covid-19.

3. Analyse the harassment, grievance and complaints handling policies and procedures available on the Career Training website to determine if they are tailored to address the potential risks related to Covid-19.

4. Conduct a risk assessment and identify the risks associated with Covid-19 in the following areas: staff shortage, financial loss, discrimination, and communication.

5. Develop a risk management plan to enable Career Training to treat identified risks. The risk management plan should include appropriate strategies to mitigate the risks identified, assign responsibility, set timelines and be cost-effective.

6. Develop a risk register outlining the identified risks, sources of risk, impact and likelihood on the Career Training organization, and treatment strategies proposed in the risk management plan. This should include the current status of risk management, agreed timeframe, and responsible persons for treatment.

7. Evaluate the risk management plan and risk register created and determine whether they effectively address the risks associated with Covid-19. Provide recommendations on how the plan could be improved.

8. Finally, summarise the key findings of your risk management assessment and outline the benefits to the Career Training organization of having such a process in place.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
In this activity, you need to prepare a risk management plan to establish the risk context by evaluating organisational processes, procedures, strengths and weaknesses of Career Training.

The risk management plan will include the following: 

List of stakeholders
Organisational process, procedures and requirements for performing risk management
Risks identified and their likelihood
Risks prioritisation and treatment information
Risks mitigation, elimination techniques
Strength and weakness of the organisational operations
Risk Analysis
To complete the risk management plan, you need to perform a SWOT analysis and a PESTLE analysis (template provided) on the organisation and, based upon the information collected, you need to complete the risk management plan template provided. 

Use the following templates to complete your assessment and upload BOTH for grading.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
SWOT Analysis Template:

Strengths:
- Nationally recognised and specialised courses
- Private RTO based in Geraldton, Western Australia with multiple satellite offices
- Eligible to apply for Australian, state and territory funding to deliver VET
- Offers qualifications up to Vocational Graduate Diploma

Weaknesses:
- Relatively small organization with limited resources
- Vulnerable to financial loss due to Covid-19 impacts
- May experience communication challenges with staff working remotely

Opportunities:
- Increased demand for online learning due to Covid-19
- Potential to expand course offerings to meet emerging industry needs
- Growing demand for vocational education and training in Australia

Threats:
- Uncertainty surrounding the ongoing impact of Covid-19 on the education industry
- Potential for increased competition from other RTOs offering similar courses
- Changes in government funding or regulatory requirements

PESTLE Analysis Template:

Political: 
- Government funding and policy changes can impact the availability of funding for vocational education and training
- Changes in legislation may affect the delivery or accreditation of courses

Economic: 
- Covid-19 has significantly impacted the economy, potentially leading to decreased demand for vocational education and training
- Financial insecurity can make it difficult for students to pay for courses or for RTOs to continue operations

Social: 
- Increased demand for vocational education and training due to changing industry needs
- Covid-19 has led to increased demand for online learning, which may be an opportunity for Career Training

Technological:
- Evolution of digital learning technologies provide for innovative delivery of training and programs
- RTOs need to keep pace with technological advancements to continue to deliver quality training

Legal: 
- Regulations and changes in legislation can impact the delivery and accreditation of courses
- Obligation to ensure a safe and secure learning environment for staff and students

Environmental: 
- Not applicable to this organisation.
 

RISK MANAGEMENT PLAN

Company name: Career Training

Description (Company) and scope of the risk management plan, including critical success factors, goals or objectives: This risk management plan has been developed to conduct an assessment of the risks associated with the Covid-19 pandemic in order to establish an effective plan for treating those risks. The critical success factors and objectives are to maintain financial stability, protect staff and students from Covid-19, and to sustain the delivery of high-quality training programs.

Context (Identify political, economic, social, legal, technological and policy context): 
- Political: Career Training is subject to government funding which can be affected by external political factors and policy changes. 
- Economic: The Covid-19 pandemic has had a significant impact on the economy, resulting in financial insecurity for Career Training and students. 
- Social: Career Training has faced increased demand for vocational education and training due to changing industry needs. 
- Technological: Career Training must keep pace with technological advancements in order to deliver high-quality training programs. 
- Legal: There are regulations and legal obligations on the organisation to ensure safe learning environments for staff and students.

Stakeholders (Internal and external): 
Internal stakeholders include Career Training staff. External stakeholders include students, government funding and regulatory bodies, and partner organisations.

Identify organisational processes, procedures and requirements for undertaking risk management: 
The following processes and procedures will be used to undertake risk management:
- Risk assessment and analysis
- Identify risks and their likelihood
- Prioritizing risks and developing treatment strategies
- Implement mitigation/elimination techniques
- Monitor and review risks regularly

Risks (Identified): 
- Staff shortage due to illness or quarantine
- Financial loss due to decreased enrolments
- Discrimination related to Covid-19 cases or preventive measures
- Communication challenges from remote working

Risk prioritisation and treatment information:
- Staff shortage: prioritized as high risk. Treatment: Implement a flexible work arrangement and cross-training programs to prevent staffing shortage.
- Financial loss: prioritized as high risk. Treatment: Obtain short-term government funding, reduced course fees for students.
- Discrimination: prioritized as moderate risk. Treatment: Conduct regular staff training on anti-discrimination measures.
- Communication: prioritized as low risk. Treatment: Develop new communication channels with staff and students.

Risk mitigation, elimination techniques:
- Staff shortage: Cross-training programs, flexible arrangements with telecommuting options.
- Financial loss: Short-term funding, reduced course fees to encourage enrolments.
- Discrimination: Regular staff training on diversity, inclusion and equal opportunity.
- Communication: Develop improved channels of communication with staff and students.

RISK ASSESSMENT AND ANALYSIS:
Based on the results of the SWOT and PESTLE analyses, the potential risks associated with Covid-19 were determined, prioritised and treated accordingly. Career Training has developed mitigation and elimination techniques to avoid and minimise the risk of staffing shortages, financial loss, discrimination and communication challenges. Regular risk assessments will be conducted to analyse and address any new emerging risks to protect staff and students and ensure compliance with legal and regulatory framework.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
PESTLE Analysis for Career Training:

Political:
Factor: Changes in government funding and policy affecting vocational education and training.
Business impact: Career Training may lose its eligibility for funding or experience reduced funding.
 
Economic:
Factor: Financial insecurity and decrease in demand for vocational education and training due to Covid-19 pandemic.
Business impact: Career Training may experience a decrease in enrolments and financial loss, and may need to adjust its prices and operations.

Sociological:
Factor: Increased demand for vocational education and training due to changing industry needs.
Business impact: Career Training has an opportunity to meet the emerging needs of the market and expand its course offerings.

Technological:
Factor: Advances in digital learning technologies offer new ways to deliver training and programs.
Business impact: Career Training needs to keep pace with technological advancements to continue delivering high-quality training programs.

Legal:
Factor: Regulations and legal obligations to ensure a safe and secure learning environment for staff and students.
Business impact: Career Training must maintain compliance with legal and regulatory frameworks relating to vocational education and training.

Environmental:
Factor: Not applicable to this organisation.

Use this template to complete this task: Company name: 
1.Objective	 2. Actions	3. Outcome	4. Resources	5. KPIs

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Company name: Career Training

1. Objective: Develop and implement a Covid-19 contingency plan to mitigate identified risks.
   Actions: Conduct a risk assessment specific to Covid-19, review and modify existing policies and procedures, implement new policies and procedures as required, communicate the contingency plan to all stakeholders.
   Outcome: Established and implemented Covid-19 contingency plan to mitigate identified risks, reducing impact on staff and students, and ensuring compliance with legal and regulatory frameworks.
   Resources: CEO, HR manager, relevant staff, external consultant as required.
   KPIs: Completion of risk assessment and contingency plan within 30 days, communication of plan to all stakeholders, regular monitoring and review of the plan.

2. Objective: Improve staff retention and support.
   Actions: Collect feedback from staff, provide resources and support as required, review and modify policies and procedures related to staff retention.
   Outcome: Improved staff satisfaction and retention, reduced turnover, increased productivity and quality of service.
   Resources: CEO, HR team, relevant staff, external consultant as required.
   KPIs: Feedback collected and reviewed within 60 days, implementation of new policies and procedures within 90 days, decreased staff turnover rate.

3. Objective: Implement a comprehensive induction plan.
   Actions: Develop and implement a new induction plan, communicate the plan to all new staff, review and modify the plan as required.
   Outcome: New staff have a clear understanding of important policies, roles and responsibilities, and the organisation's culture, leading to increased productivity and job satisfaction.
   Resources: HR team, relevant staff, external consultant as required.
   KPIs: Roll-out of induction program within 15 days, positive feedback from new staff on induction program.

4. Objective: Improve communication between staff and management.
   Actions: Review communication channels and identify areas for improvement, develop and implement new communication channels as required, encourage regular feedback from staff.
   Outcome: Improved communication between staff and management leading to a more positive workplace culture, increased productivity and job satisfaction.
   Resources: CEO, HR team, relevant staff, external consultant as required.
   KPIs: Identification of areas for improvement within 30 days, implementation of new communication channels within 60 days, regular feedback collected and reviewed.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Note when wording your KPI's that they must be measurable!

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Thank you for the feedback. Below are the revised KPIs for the reprioritised operational plan:

1. Objective: Develop and implement a Covid-19 contingency plan to mitigate identified risks.
   Actions: Conduct a risk assessment specific to Covid-19, review and modify existing policies and procedures, implement new policies and procedures as required, communicate the contingency plan to all stakeholders.
   Outcome: Established and implemented Covid-19 contingency plan to mitigate identified risks, reducing impact on staff and students, and ensuring compliance with legal and regulatory frameworks.
   Resources: CEO, HR manager, relevant staff, external consultant as required.
   KPIs: Completion of Covid-19 risk assessment and contingency plan within 30 days, communication plan to all stakeholders within 7 days, review of the contingency plan within 90 days to ensure effectiveness.

2. Objective: Improve staff retention and support.
   Actions: Collect feedback from staff, provide resources and support as required, review and modify policies and procedures related to staff retention.
   Outcome: Improved staff satisfaction and retention, reduced turnover, increased productivity and quality of service.
   Resources: CEO, HR team, relevant staff, external consultant as required.
   KPIs: Feedback survey collected and reviewed within 60 days, implementation of one new policy or procedure within 90 days to address staff feedback.
